name: Mexican Fiesta Breakfast Casserole
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C). Generously spray an 8-inch square glass baking dish with cooking spray to prevent sticking.

2. Heat a medium skillet over medium-high heat. Add the Chorizo, breaking it up with a wooden spoon. Cook, stirring frequently, until browned and crumbly with no pink remaining, about 3 to 5 minutes. Transfer to a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess grease.

3. In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the warm Water and Instant Dry Milk until the milk powder is fully dissolved. Add the cooked chorizo, torn Tortillas, beaten Eggs, drained Pinto Beans, diced Tomatoes with Green Chile Peppers with their juices, frozen Corn, chopped Green Chile Peppers, shredded Pepper Jack Cheese, diced Bell Pepper, and sliced Scallion. Stir everything together until well combined.

4. Pour the mixture into the prepared baking dish, spreading it evenly. Bake in the preheated oven until the casserole is golden brown on top and the center is fully set and no longer jiggly, about 55 to 60 minutes.

5. Remove from the oven and immediately spread the Sour Cream evenly over the top. Sprinkle with the Mexican Cheese Blend. Return to the oven just until the cheese melts, about 1 minute. Let the casserole cool for 5 to 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
simpleInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven and spray the baking dish with cooking spray.

2. Cook the Chorizo until browned and crumbly, then drain on paper towels.

3. Mix the warm Water with Instant Dry Milk, then combine all ingredients in a large bowl.

4. Pour the mixture into the dish and bake until golden and set.

5. Top with Sour Cream and Mexican Cheese Blend, then melt briefly.
